    Home › Records › The fastest aircraft here

    The fastest aircraft here

    Ranked by maximum speed — where the supersonics leave everything else standing.

    Military and supersonic figures are maximum speed; airliner figures are maximum operating speed, which is faster than the cruise they actually fly. Comparing them is unavoidable, so it is stated plainly.

    1 North American XB-70 Valkyrie3,219 km/h 2,000 mphPreserved — 1 of 2 survives 2 Tupolev Tu-1442,285 km/h 1,420 mphRetired 3 Tupolev Tu-1602,220 km/h 1,379 mphIn service — production restarted 4 Concorde2,179 km/h 1,354 mphRetired 2003 5 Boeing B-52 Stratofortress1,047 km/h 651 mphIn service since 1955 — planned to 2050s 6 Boeing 747988 km/h 614 mphOut of production (2023) — in service 7 Douglas DC-8965 km/h 600 mphRetired (a handful of specials) 8 Boeing 787 Dreamliner954 km/h 593 mphIn production 9 Boeing 727953 km/h 592 mphRetired from airline service 10 Boeing 777950 km/h 590 mphIn production (777X in certification) 11 Boeing 777X950 km/h 590 mphIn certification — deliveries expected 2027 12 Ilyushin Il-86950 km/h 590 mphRetired 13 Airbus A380945 km/h 587 mphOut of production (2021) — in service 14 Airbus A350945 km/h 587 mphIn production 15 McDonnell Douglas MD-11945 km/h 587 mphIn service (freighters) 16 Vickers VC10935 km/h 581 mphRetired 2013 17 Lockheed C-5 Galaxy919 km/h 571 mphIn service — USAF, modernised as C-5M 18 Boeing 757918 km/h 570 mphOut of production (2004) — in service 19 Airbus A340913 km/h 567 mphOut of production (2011) — in service 20 Airbus A330913 km/h 567 mphIn production (A330neo) 21 Boeing 767913 km/h 567 mphIn production (freighter/tanker) 22 McDonnell Douglas DC-10908 km/h 564 mphRetired (special missions) 23 Ilyushin Il-96900 km/h 559 mphIn limited service 24 Ilyushin Il-62900 km/h 559 mphNearly retired 25 Ilyushin Il-76900 km/h 559 mphIn production since 1971 26 Airbus A300897 km/h 557 mphOut of production (2007) — in service 27 Lockheed L-1011 TriStar890 km/h 553 mphRetired (one rocket launcher) 28 Boeing 747 Dreamlifter878 km/h 546 mphIn service — 4 built 29 Boeing 737876 km/h 544 mphIn production (MAX series) 30 Airbus A320 family871 km/h 541 mphIn production 31 Airbus A220871 km/h 541 mphIn production 32 Tupolev Tu-114870 km/h 541 mphRetired 33 Embraer E-Jet E2870 km/h 541 mphIn production 34 COMAC C919870 km/h 541 mphIn service — production ramping 35 Antonov An-124 Ruslan865 km/h 537 mphIn service 36 Antonov An-225 Mriya850 km/h 528 mphDestroyed (2022) — one built 37 Airbus Beluga850 km/h 528 mphIn service 38 Boeing C-17 Globemaster III830 km/h 516 mphIn service worldwide 39 Antonov An-22 Antei740 km/h 460 mphRetired 40 De Havilland Canada Dash 8-400667 km/h 414 mphIn service 41 ATR 72510 km/h 317 mphIn production 42 Bristol Brabazon483 km/h 300 mphPrototype only — scrapped 1953 43 Hughes H-4 Hercules402 km/h 250 mphPreserved — flew once, 1947

    43 aircraft ranked · every figure sourced on its own page · last verified 2026-07-14

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
    © 2026 aircraft.fyi
